name: Richie Cole
description: American jazz saxophonist, composer, and arranger (1948–2020)
abstract: Richie Cole (February 29, 1948 – May 2, 2020) was an American jazz saxophonist, composer, and arranger.

concerts.label: Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: There I Go, There I Go, There I Go - We Remember Eddie Jefferson

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I got many of those guys like Richie Cole and, you know, they were all friends of mine and had been with me with the Dues Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 27, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: And the best year we had was, um in the sax section was Richie Cole, Mike Brecker and Roger Rosenburg... And I had a band there
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: And he had said that we’re going to go into the Vanguard and this and that and everything was fine, Richie Cole and all this and that. ... I called Richie Cole. So he said, “Danny, you missed it by 30 seconds.”

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Woody Herman) only responds to the interviewer's mention of Richie Cole; there is no direct interaction or personal relationship described between Herman and Cole in the transcript.

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: I met guys that were my age but they were heavy hitters: Jon Faddis, Richie Cole, and I got to interact with them and talk to them and sit in with them
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, November 11, 2017 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: Kimery: It reminds me. I saw Charles McPherson play. He invited Richie Cole up. Charles played. Richie walked off the stage afterwards.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
